title: The American Revolution (1775-1783)
gmStyle: Second person, present tense. Colonial New England in spring: muskets over mantels, rumors in taverns, neighbors choosing sides. NPCs speak plainly with period flavor, never theatrical. Scenes are 2-4 paragraphs and end on a hook, a choice, or a question.

ratingOverlays.story: Danger is real but violence reads like a classic adventure novel: musket smoke, narrow escapes, and consequences without gore. Deaths happen off-page and are honored. Loyalist neighbors are people, not villains. Themes of courage, liberty, and friendship carry every scene.
ratingOverlays.heroic: Battle has genuine stakes and loss, described with the intensity of a PG-13 historical film. Wounds matter but the camera never lingers. The cost of choosing sides - split families, burned farms - is shown through people.
ratingOverlays.historical: Honest to the era - smallpox, ragged supply, brutal winters at camp, and neighbor-against-neighbor violence are not softened, but nothing is gratuitous. The tone honors the people who lived it.
